rationale: Reconstructed det.rs with imports, verus! wrapper, and minimal type definitions (AbstractEndPoint, EndPoint, HashMap) needed to parse and verify the synthetic obligation locally. The LLM PROOF BLOCK uses an `assert(... ==> ...) by { ... }` block that case-splits on the premise of the implication; inside the branch, it asserts view-level extensional equality of the two posts (`post1_self_@ =~= post2_self_@`), then struct-level extensional equality (`post1_self_ =~= post2_self_`), then notes `r1 == r2` (both are `()`). No forbidden constructs (assume/admit/external_body/etc.) appear inside the LLM PROOF BLOCK. Verus exits 0 with `1 verified, 0 errors`.
